title: digitalis erubescens
intro: Digitalis erubescens, also known as foxglove, is a biennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to slow heart rate and increase its efficiency. The leaves of Digitalis erubescens contain cardiac glycosides, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing symptoms of heart failure and regulating blood pressure. In herbal preparations, Digitalis erubescens is often used to create tinctures, infusions, and extracts, which can be administered orally or topically under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
